creator: yds@ingress.net
text:
I had this problem right after KDE 1.0 came out. The problem is KDM doesn't
set the path right and can't find kwm and friends. The problem went away
after I put a usable PATH in ~/.xinitrc (or whatever, I don't remember, it's
been working for a while :). Don't know if there's a fix or if this should
just be documented somewhere. Since pre 1.0 releases didn't need the path
explicitly set this seems to be a bug in KDM at least on FreeBSD.

-----Original Message-----
From: jrista@hotmail.com <jrista@hotmail.com>
To: freebsd-gnats-submit@FreeBSD.ORG <freebsd-gnats-submit@FreeBSD.ORG>
Date: Wednesday, November 18, 1998 8:44 PM
Subject: ports/8754: KDE: KDM Display Manager has login problem...wont let
users other than root log in


>
>>Number:         8754
>>Category:       ports
>>Synopsis:       KDE: KDM Display Manager has login problem...wont let
users other than root log in
>>Confidential:   no
>>Severity:       non-critical
>>Priority:       medium
>>Responsible:    freebsd-ports
>>State:          open
>>Quarter:
>>Keywords:
>>Date-Required:
>>Class:          sw-bug
>>Submitter-Id:   current-users
>>Arrival-Date:   Wed Nov 18 17:30:01 PST 1998
>>Last-Modified:
>>Originator:     Jon Rista
>>Organization:
>(none)
>>Release:        2.2.7-Stable
>>Environment:
>FreeBSD myname.my.domain 2.2.7-RELEASE FreeBSD 2.2.7-RELEASE #0: Mon Jan 1
20:19:56 MST 1996    root@myname.my.domain:/usr/src/sys/compile/BSDWITHSOUND
>>Description:
>The KDE KDM Display Manager has a login problem. After boot, login under
any name. After running KDM, only the user who originally logged in from the
command prompt can log in with the display manager. i.e. If I log in as root
only root can log in with KDM. If I log in as jrista only jrista can log in
with KDM. If I set kdm to run from a /usr/X11R6/etc/rc.d/ script (i.e.
kdm.sh*) only root can log in with KDM.
>>How-To-Repeat:
>Boot to command prompt. Log in under any name. Type kdm at the prompt. Try
to log in under a diffrent name as the one originally logged in as.
>>Fix:
>
>>Audit-Trail:
>>Unformatted:
>
>To Unsubscribe: send mail to majordomo@FreeBSD.org
>with "unsubscribe freebsd-ports" in the body of the message
